terms.definition: TP / (TP + FP): fraction of predicted positives that are truly positive at a fixed threshold. Also called positive predictive value (PPV). Canonical definition in this topic.
TP / (TP + FN): fraction of actual positives correctly identified at a fixed threshold. Also called sensitivity, true positive rate (TPR), or hit rate. Canonical definition in this topic.
Harmonic mean of precision and recall: 2 × (precision × recall) / (precision + recall). Also called F-measure or F-score. Canonical definition in this topic.
Synonym for precision—the proportion of positive predictions that are correct.
Average biased toward the smaller of two values; used in F1 so neither precision nor recall can be ignored when one is near zero.
Unweighted mean of per-class F1 scores in multiclass problems; gives equal weight to rare and frequent classes.
F1 computed from global TP, FP, and FN pooled across all classes; equals accuracy for single-label multiclass and favors frequent classes.
